stem: Line p is defined by the equation 7x - 5y = 12. Line q is parallel to line p in the xy-plane. What is the slope of line q?
rationale: Choice A is correct. Rewriting 7x - 5y = 12 in slope-intercept form: -5y = -7x + 12, so y = (7/5)x - 12/5. The slope of line p is 7/5. Parallel lines share the same slope, so the slope of line q is also 7/5. Choice B is the reciprocal. Choices C and D have the wrong sign — those would describe perpendicular slopes (with sign flipped).
